zh.lifeSummary: 當Emma de Port於1038年出生在英國諾圖波藍德阿恩維克時，她的父親Hugues de Portu Domesday lord of Basing22歲，母親Orence St. John37歲。她於1066年與Sir William de Percy Sr在英國英格蘭越可爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1100年在英國越可爾惠特比享年62歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾惠特比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Emma de Port于1038年出生在英国諾圖波藍德阿恩維克时，她的父亲Hugues de Portu Domesday lord of Basing22岁，母亲Orence St. John37岁。她于1066年与Sir William de Percy Sr在英国英格兰越可爾结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1100年在英国越可爾惠特比享年62岁，埋葬在英国英格兰越可爾惠特比。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Port
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English port(e) ‘gateway, entrance’ (Old French porte, from Latin porta), hence a topographic name for someone who lived near the gates of a fortified town or city, or for the gatekeeper. Compare Porter 1.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a harbor or in a market town, from a homonymous Middle English port(e) (Old English port ‘harbor, market town’, from Latin portus ‘harbor, haven’, reinforced in Middle English by Old French port, from the same source).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Port‐en‐Bessin (Calvados), which is recorded as Portu in 1035–36. The placename derives from Latin portus ‘port, harbour, haven’.

variants.name: Porter
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for the gatekeeper of a walled town or city, or the doorkeeper of a great house, castle, or monastery, from Middle English and Older Scots porter(e), port(o)ur ‘doorkeeper, gatekeeper’ (Anglo-Norman French port(i)er, portur, Latin portarius). The office often came with accommodation, lands, and other privileges for the bearer, and in some cases was hereditary, especially in the case of a royal castle. The name has been established in Ireland since the 13th century. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates and equivalents in other languages, for example German Pförtner (see Fortner ) and Poertner .

English: occupational name for a man who carried loads for a living, especially one who used his own muscle power rather than a beast of burden or a wheeled vehicle. This sense is from Middle English port(o)ur, porter ‘porter, carrier of burdens’ (Anglo-Norman French portur, porteo(u)r).
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized, of Poorter, status name for a freeman (burgher) of a town, Middle Dutch portere, modern Dutch poorter. Compare De Porter .

variants.name: Porth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Manfred, Beate, Horst, Siegfried, Uwe, Volker.

variants.name: Porten
meanings: English: possibly a variant of Porton, a habitational name from Porton in Wiltshire or Poorton in Dorset; both placenames are formed with an obscure first element, perhaps the name of a river, + Old English tūn ‘settlement’.
North German: variant of   Port   (inflected form after an original preposition).

nameMeanings.name: Emma
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, originally a short form of compound names such as Ermintrude containing the word erm(en), irm(en) ‘entire’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, but its popularity in medieval England was greatly enhanced by the fact that it had been borne by the mother of Edward the Confessor, herself a Norman. In modern times, it was only in moderate use early in the 20th century but rose sharply in favour in the 1970s and has since remained perennially popular.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

en.lifeSummary: When Emma de Port was born in 1038, in Alnwick, Northumberland, England, her father, Hugues de Portu Domesday lord of Basing, was 22 and her mother, Orence St. John, was 37. She married Sir William de Percy Sr in 1066, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1100, in Whitby, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 62, and was buried in Whitby, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Emma de Port est née en 1038, à Alnwick, Northumberland, Angleterre, son père, Hugues de Portu Domesday lord of Basing, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Orence St. John, avait 37 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William de Percy Sr en 1066, à Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1100, à Whitby, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterrée à Whitby, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Emma de Port 1038 in Alnwick, Northumberland, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hugues de Portu Domesday lord of Basing 22 und ihre Mutter Orence St. John 37. Sie hat 1066 in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom Sir William de Percy Sr geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1100 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Whitby, Yorkshire, England und wurde in Whitby, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lei sposato Sir William de Percy Sr nel 1066, nel Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia. ja.lifeSummary: Emma de Portが1038年にイギリスノースウンバーランドアルンウィックで生まれた時，父Hugues de Portu Domesday lord of Basingは22歳，母Orence St. Johnは37歳であった。1066年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアでSir William de Percy Srと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1100年にイギリスヨークシュアイアウィトビーで62歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアウィトビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Emma de Port(이)가 1038년 영국 노르스벌랜드 알윅에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hugues de Portu Domesday lord of Basing(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Orence St. John(은)는 37세였다. 그녀는 1066년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 Sir William de Percy Sr(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1100년 영국 요르크실르 휘트비에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 휘트비에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Emma de Port родилась в 1038 г. в местности алнвикк, нортумбирланд, Англия, её отцу по имени Hugues de Portu Domesday lord of Basing было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Orence St. John, 37. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William de Percy Sr в 1066 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1100 г. в местности уитби, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 62 лет и была похоронена в местности уитби, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.
